title: anti-legacy Domain Vocabulary
description: Validates .anti-legacy/vocabulary.json — the config-driven domain vocabulary mined from the wicked-estate code graph by scripts/vocabulary.py and enriched by the extraction agent against skills/vocabulary/SKILL.md. Two ORTHOGONAL axes per term: `status` (proposed|confirmed = is the TERM a real domain item) and `verification` (unverified|untrusted_verified|trusted_verified = is the term's DEFINITION/MEANING proven against CODE LOGIC). Bootstrap emits status=proposed + verification=unverified with a BLANK definition; only trusted_verified definitions may be asserted as fact in a rule statement.

bootstrap_run.description: Run-level metadata for the latest bootstrap (db/ts/min_freq) — lives ONCE here, not per term.

canonical.description: Interned term key, unique within terms[].

term_type.description: Which miner / usage produced the term.

definition.description: Plain-language meaning. BLANK when proposed-by-bootstrap until authored. Only assert a trusted_verified definition as fact.

aliases.description: Other spellings meaning the SAME canonical (mined field-name variants + human additions).

pseudonyms_slang.description: Informal / tribal / business-side names a human attaches; never bootstrap-coined.

status.description: Lifecycle of whether the TERM is real. Bootstrap emits proposed; a human/agent promotes to confirmed.

verification.description: Trust spectrum over the DEFINITION. unverified=mined/claim-only; untrusted_verified=doc/convention-corroborated; trusted_verified=proven against CODE LOGIC or ratified by a human SME.

freq.description: TRUE recurrence count of the term across the mined kinds. Always present; the per-term evidence index lives in wicked-estate (`query`/`rank`), not here.

mined_from.description: Which miner proposed the term (estate_behavior_kinds | behavior_kinds | field-token). Absent on purely human-added terms.

sources.description: Optional inline evidence. EMPTY by default — where-used belongs to wicked-estate. Populated only when config.vocabulary.max_sources_per_term opts in, or by human-attached doc/SME citations.

provenance.description: DEPRECATED per-term block (run metadata moved to meta.bootstrap_run); still permitted for backward-compat with older files.

kind.description: graph_node = mined from a code-graph node (CODE LOGIC ground truth); doc = a README/comment CLAIM (unproven); human = SME confirmation.

ref.description: For graph_node: the full SymbolId. For doc: file#anchor. For human: author@date.

note.description: e.g. 'CLAIM — unconfirmed against code' for a doc source.
